content.text: Answer four problems. (If you turn in more, the first four will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(R=\mathbb{Z}[i]\) be the ring of Gaussian integers. Let \(M\) be a maximal ideal of \(R\). Prove that \(R/M\) is a finite field of order \(p\) or \(p^2\) where \(p\) is a (rational) prime number.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Give an example of an integral domain which is not a unique factorization domain.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example of a principal ideal domain which is not a field and is not isomorphic to \(\mathbb{Z}\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Give an example of a commutative ring with identity which is not an integral domain.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that every Euclidean domain is a principal ideal domain.

body.text: Let \(R\) be a ring with identity and let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module. Prove that the annihilator \(\operatorname{Ann}(M)\) is a two sided ideal of \(R\).
Let \(V\) be a vector space of dimension 4 over the field \(\mathbb{Q}\) of rational numbers. Let \(T:V\to V\) be a linear transformation. Suppose that \((T^2-\operatorname{Id})^2=0\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: What are the possible minimal polynomials for \(T\)?

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: For each possible minimal polynomial from (a), give a matrix with this minimal polynomial.

body.text: Let \(F/K\) be an extension of fields, and suppose that \([F:K]=5\). Prove that there exists an irreducible polynomial \(p(x)\in K[x]\) such that \(F\) is isomorphic to \(K[x]/(p(x))\).
